intent: recipe app for people who hate cooking

body.intent: recipe app for people who hate cooking

draft.title: recipe app for people who hate cooking
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
recipe app for people who hate cooking

Building your first draft…

data.intent: recipe app for people who hate cooking

facade.hypothesis: Users who hate cooking prefer gentle, forgiving UI that feels approachable over intimidating
facade.label: Soft rounded buttons
facade.acceptImplies: Design should prioritize warmth and accessibility; avoid harsh, clinical interfaces
facade.rejectImplies: Consider bolder, more geometric or angular UI; users may prefer clarity over softness

facade.hypothesis: Users who hate cooking prefer interfaces that feel forgiving and low-pressure—soft visual language reduces kitchen anxiety
facade.axisTargeted: warm vs cool, organic vs digital
facade.label: Soft blur effect
facade.acceptImplies: The app should use gentle gradients, rounded corners, and diffused backgrounds to feel approachable and non-intimidating
facade.rejectImplies: Users prefer sharper, more direct visual language—crisp typography, clear boundaries, and high contrast to feel in control

evidence.hypothesis: Users who hate cooking prefer gentle, forgiving UI that feels approachable over intimidating
evidence.implication: Design should prioritize warmth and accessibility; avoid harsh, clinical interfaces

facade.hypothesis: Users want ingredient quantities upfront without reading prose
facade.label: Just the numbers
facade.acceptImplies: Recipe app should lead with ingredient list as scannable metrics (amounts, units) before any instructions or flavor descriptions
facade.rejectImplies: Users need narrative context and story around recipes—quantities alone feel cold or confusing without method or why

facade.hypothesis: Showing recipe ingredients via smooth animated entrance rather than instant load reduces cognitive overwhelm for cooking-averse users
facade.axisTargeted: Motion and transition style — fluid reveal vs instant appearance
facade.label: Ingredients fade in
facade.acceptImplies: User values gentle, paced onboarding to recipe steps; prefers motion that builds anticipation; likely wants calm, unjudgmental UX for non-cooks
facade.rejectImplies: User wants immediate, unadorned information; finds animation unnecessary or patronizing; prefers efficient, zero-friction access to recipe data

facade.hypothesis: Users who hate cooking respond better to a cheeky, sarcastic AI assistant tone rather than a neutral or overly cheerful one
facade.axisTargeted: Voice and personality — playful vs serious
facade.label: Sassy robot voice
facade.acceptImplies: The app's core voice should be irreverent, witty, and mock the cooking process itself ('Ugh, chopping? Let me do that for you')
facade.rejectImplies: Users prefer either neutral, instructional tone OR warm, encouraging tone — not sarcasm or attitude

facade.hypothesis: Users who hate cooking prefer large, scannable recipe formats over dense text—reducing cognitive load makes the app feel less intimidating
facade.label: Giant recipe cards
facade.acceptImplies: Spacious, large-text layouts work better than compact views. Consider card-based UI with plenty of whitespace. Users may also prefer big, readable ingredient lists and simple step breakdowns.
facade.rejectImplies: Dense layouts aren't the barrier. Spaciousness alone won't solve friction. The problem might be interaction model (how they navigate), information hierarchy, or visual complexity rather than text size.

axes.label: Whether user wants visual simplicity/minimalism vs. rich imagery/inspiration; reveals tolerance for food photography and visual complexity
Whether user prefers guided step-by-step flows vs. ingredient-first browsing; reveals navigation preference for cooking-averse users
Whether user responds to encouraging/motivational tone vs. straightforward/no-nonsense tone; reveals personality fit for cooking-resistant audience
Whether user wants to feel empowered/capable vs. relieved/off-the-hook; reveals emotional need driving the app choice
Whether user wants detailed explanations/why-it-works vs. just the essentials/quick facts; reveals information appetite for cooking-averse users
Whether user wants instant gratification/quick feedback vs. patient pacing; reveals tolerance for complexity in a tool meant to reduce friction
axes.poleA: Fast and snappy or take your time?

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants visual simplicity/minimalism vs. rich imagery/inspiration; reveals tolerance for food photography and visual complexity
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to encouraging/motivational tone vs. straightforward/no-nonsense tone; reveals personality fit for cooking-resistant audience
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants to feel empowered/capable vs. relieved/off-the-hook; reveals emotional need driving the app choice
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants detailed explanations/why-it-works vs. just the essentials/quick facts; reveals information appetite for cooking-averse users
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants instant gratification/quick feedback vs. patient pacing; reveals tolerance for complexity in a tool meant to reduce friction
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Users who hate cooking prefer soft, inviting color palettes over stark whites or dark interfaces, creating a sense of comfort that counters kitchen anxiety
facade.label: Warm cream backgrounds
facade.acceptImplies: Design should use warm neutrals (cream, soft beige, warm grey) to create a calming, non-clinical cooking environment
facade.rejectImplies: Users may prefer cooler tones, higher contrast, or modern minimalism—suggesting they want clarity and efficiency over warmth

draft.title: QuickBite – Recipe App for People Who Hate Cooking
draft.summary: A recipe app that strips away complexity and delivers dead-simple meals in 15 minutes or less, with ingredient lists you can actually find and zero pretentious cooking jargon.
draft.html:
QuickBite

Meals so simple, you'll actually make them

Today's Picks

Garlic Butter Pasta
12 min • 4 ingredients
Sheet Pan Chicken
15 min • 5 ingredients
Egg Fried Rice
10 min • 6 ingredients

What's in Your Kitchen?

Tell us what you have, we'll suggest meals

Browse by Mood

I'm Starving (5 min)
Instant noodle hacks, toast toppers
I Have 15 Minutes
Stir-fries, quick pasta, sheet pan meals
I'm Lazy (Minimal Cleanup)
One-pot, one-pan, no-cook options
